methodology_sections: 1.2 Trust Boundary Analysis - Internal/external/privileged boundaries, validation levels
2.2 State Transition Mapping - Critical state variables, invariants, conditional paths
3.1 Vulnerability Pattern Matching - SWC patterns, anti-patterns, missing safeguards

failure_modes: Validator creates invalid assertion leading to slashing
Validator goes offline causing rollup to halt until AFK timeout
attack_vectors: Sybil attack if whitelist is disabled (mitigated by stake requirements)
Spamming invalid assertions to delay confirmation (mitigated by exponential cost of stakes)

failure_modes: Compromised fast confirmer confirms invalid state transition
Fast confirmer creates multiple sibling assertions causing incorrect escrow accounting
attack_vectors: Key compromise of the AnyTrust committee leading to total rollup takeover

failure_modes: Premature confirmation rejected due to insufficient unrivaled time
attack_vectors: Providing fake bisection roots (prevented by prefix proof verification)
Front-running bisections (mitigated by deterministic bisection heights and merge moves)

conditions: Inbox accounting is strictly monotonic and valid
invariants: Only one stake is required per lineage
Sibling assertions transfer requiredStake to loserStakeEscrow

conditions: If siblings exist, assertion must be the winner of the challenge manager
invariants: Only one child of a parent can be confirmed
Confirmed state updates the outbox send root

conditions: Edge has been unrivaled for challengePeriodBlocks OR
Edge is length 1 and one-step proof is valid
invariants: Only one rival edge can be confirmed
Confirmed edge permanently sets status to Confirmed

vulnerability_patterns.evidence: The anyTrustFastConfirmer can bypass all fraud proof mechanisms and confirm arbitrary state transitions.
vulnerability_patterns.exploitation_method: If the private keys of the AnyTrust committee are compromised, an attacker can instantly confirm a malicious state, stealing all bridged assets.

vulnerability_patterns.pattern: Denial of Service / Permanent State Change
vulnerability_patterns.evidence: If the rollup is paused by the admin for VALIDATOR_AFK_BLOCKS (~28 days), the _validatorIsAfk() condition becomes true because block.number continues to increase.
vulnerability_patterns.exploitation_method: Anyone can call removeWhitelistAfterValidatorAfk() to permanently disable the validator whitelist. This is intended for abandoned rollups, but interacts poorly with the Pausable mechanism.

vulnerability_patterns.evidence: As documented in comments: 'if the losing staker have staked more than requiredStake, the excess stake will be stuck'.
vulnerability_patterns.exploitation_method: A validator who deposits more than the required stake and subsequently loses a challenge will never become an 'inactive staker', permanently locking their excess stake in the contract.

privileged_operations.notes: Bypasses challenge periods to instantly confirm assertions
Creates and instantly confirms an assertion
Callable by anyone if the L1 chain ID changes
Callable by anyone if no assertions are confirmed for VALIDATOR_AFK_BLOCKS
